title: Vue.js Methods Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of defining functions, access to 'this', and avoiding arrow functions.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary role of a Method in Vue.js?
options: To define the logic for event handling, data manipulation, or reusable functions.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.
To replace the standard HTML input elements.

questions.question: Which option is used to define Methods in a Vue instance?
How do you access a data property from within a Vue method?
Why is it generally discouraged to use Arrow Functions when defining Vue methods?
options: They don't have their own 'this' context, which is required to access the Vue instance data.
They are only for functional components.

questions.question: Can you use methods inside templates (e.g. {{ formatName() }})?
options: Yes, but they will be called on every render.
No, only data properties are allowed.
